goal.ko: 장소를 설명하고 문화 이야기를 나눌 수 있어요.
goal.en: I can describe places and share cultural stories.

script.speaker: grandpa
script.text: 내일 할머니랑 할아버지 용문사에 가는데 같이 갈 사람?
script.en: Grandma and Grandpa are going to Yongmunsa tomorrow — who wants to come along?

script.speaker: grandpa
script.en: It's a very old Buddhist temple.

script.speaker: grandpa
script.en: If you go to Yongmunsa, there's a ginkgo tree that's over 1,100 years old.

annotation.note:
동사 + (으)면 = if / when
용문사에 가면 = If/when you go to Yongmunsa
받침 O → 으면  |  받침 X / ㄹ → 면
먹으면 / 가면 / 만들면

script.speaker: grandma
script.text: 신라의 마지막 왕자님 마의 태자가 꽂은 지팡이였대.
script.en: They say it was the walking stick planted by Prince Maui, the last prince of Silla.

script.speaker: kayo
script.en: It's that old and still alive?

script.speaker: grandma
script.en: Of course! It's a magical tree that guards the temple.

script.speaker: riam
script.en: Wow! I'm going to make a wish to the ginkgo tree.

annotation.note:
한테 = to / for (person or animal)
은행나무한테 부탁할 거야
= going to ask the ginkgo tree
↔ 에게 (formal) = 한테 (informal)
한테서 = from (a person/animal)

script.speaker: riam
script.en: Please protect our grandma and grandpa.

annotation.note:
잖아요 = [It's true,] you know / right?
늙었잖아요 = They're old, you know!
= 상대방도 알고 있는 사실 강조
했잖아요 = I did it, you know! (reminder)

items.english: Prince Maui — the last prince of Silla kingdom

items.note: 사람에게는 쓰지 않아요 — use 늙다 for people

items.english: to be old (of people, living things)

grammar.title: -(으)ㄹ + 명사
grammar.explanation_en: The third and final 관형절 form — completing the trilogy! While -(으)ㄴ describes completed actions and -는 describes ongoing ones, -(으)ㄹ describes future, intended, or potential actions. Think: 'the person who WILL go / something I'm GOING TO do.' See L2 ep01 for the full -(으)ㄴ/는/ㄹ comparison table.

scene_example.english: Anyone who wants to come along? (lit. 'a person who will go together')

rule_boxes.label: Vowel or ㄹ stem → -ㄹ + Noun
rule_boxes.content: 가다 → 갈 사람 · 오다 → 올 사람 · 살다 → 살 곳
rule_boxes.note: 같이 갈 사람? = a person who will go together

rule_boxes.label: Consonant stem → -을 + Noun
rule_boxes.content: 먹다 → 먹을 것 · 읽다 → 읽을 책 · 찾다 → 찾을 길
rule_boxes.note: 먹을 것 = something to eat / that I'll eat

rule_boxes.label: The full trilogy · 관형절 3형 비교
rule_boxes.content: -(으)ㄴ (past/adj) → 간 사람 · -는 (present) → 가는 사람 · -(으)ㄹ (future) → 갈 사람
rule_boxes.note: See L2 ep01 for the full comparison table and drill

items.en: a person who will go together

grammar.title: -는데
grammar.explanation_en: Like saying 'So we're going to X, and...' — -는데 gives background context before making a request, asking a question, or introducing a contrast. It doesn't finish the thought — it sets the stage.
grammar.pattern: Action verb stem + -는데 · Descriptive verb/adj + -(으)ㄴ데 · Noun + -인데

scene_example.korean: 내일 용문사에 가는데 같이 갈 사람?
scene_example.english: We're going to Yongmunsa tomorrow — anyone want to come along?

rule_boxes.label: Action verb + -는데
rule_boxes.content: 가다 → 가는데 · 먹다 → 먹는데 · 살다 → 사는데
rule_boxes.note: 용문사에 가는데 같이 갈 사람? = We're going — anyone coming?

rule_boxes.label: Adj / descriptive verb + -(으)ㄴ데 · Noun + -인데
rule_boxes.note: 날씨가 추운데 따뜻하게 입어요 = It's cold, so dress warmly

rule_boxes.label: What comes after -는데?
rule_boxes.content: 1. Request: 배고픈데 뭐 먹을까? · 2. Question: 숙제가 있는데 어때요? · 3. Contrast: 맛있는데 비싸요.
rule_boxes.note: The second part can be a question, suggestion, or surprise — context decides!

examples.en: I'm busy tomorrow — can you help?
It's raining — do you have an umbrella?

fill_blanks.sentence: 오늘 약속이 있___ 같이 갈 수 있어요?

grammar.title: -에게 / -한테
grammar.explanation_en: Marks the target of your action — who you're giving something to, asking, requesting, or talking to. -한테 is casual spoken Korean; -에게 is more formal and used in writing.

scene_example.english: I'm going to make a request to the ginkgo tree.

rule_boxes.label: -한테 · casual, spoken
rule_boxes.content: 친구한테 물어봐 · 선생님한테 말해 · 강아지한테 줘
rule_boxes.note: 은행나무한테 부탁할 거야 = I'm going to ask the tree (casual)

rule_boxes.label: -에게 · formal, written
rule_boxes.note: Used in letters, news, formal speech — same meaning as -한테

rule_boxes.label: FROM a person → -한테서 / -에게서
rule_boxes.note: -에서 is for places · -한테서/-에게서 is for people & animals

examples.en: I gave food to the dog.
I received a gift from a friend.

grammar.title: -잖아요
grammar.explanation_en: Used when reminding someone of something they already know, or pointing out something obvious — often with a touch of playfulness or mild frustration. 'You know it's true!'

rule_boxes.label: -잖아요 = 'you know!' (shared fact)
rule_boxes.note: Speaker assumes the listener already knows or should know this fact

rule_boxes.label: Tone: teasing, reminding, or surprised
rule_boxes.content: 어려운 거 알면서 왜 물어봐? → 어려운 거잖아! ('You know it's hard!')
rule_boxes.note: 할머니 할아버지도 늙었잖아요 = a cheeky but affectionate observation 😄

examples.en: We don't have class today, you know.
That restaurant is good, you know!

banmal_jondaemal.intro_ko: 이번 에피소드에서는 할아버지·할머니(어른) → 리암·카요(아이)는 반말, 리암·카요(아이) → 어른들은 존댓말을 쓰는 자연스러운 패턴이 잘 드러나요.
banmal_jondaemal.intro_en: In this episode, grandpa/grandma use casual speech (반말) to the kids, and the kids use polite speech (존댓말) to the grandparents — the most natural Korean family dynamic!

rows.english: Anyone who wants to come along?
There's a ginkgo tree over 1,100 years old.
Of course. It's a magical tree that guards the temple.

adult_dialogue.title_en: Making Temple Trip Plans with a Friend
adult_dialogue.setup_ko: 친한 친구 사이지만 아직 격식체 — 한국 성인들은 처음엔 존댓말로 시작해요.
adult_dialogue.setup_en: Close friends, but still using polite speech — Korean adults default to formal until they agree to drop it.

lines.speaker: 친구 A
lines.text: 이번 주말에 용문사에 가는데 같이 갈래요?
lines.en: I'm heading to Yongmunsa this weekend — want to come along?

lines.speaker: 친구 B
lines.en: Yongmunsa? That's where the famous ginkgo tree is, right!

lines.speaker: 친구 A
lines.text: 맞아요. 1,100살이 넘는 나무래요. 한 번쯤은 봐야 할 것 같아서요.
lines.en: Exactly. They say it's over 1,100 years old. Felt like I should see it at least once.

lines.speaker: 친구 B
lines.text: 저도 꼭 가 보고 싶었는데 잘됐네요! 몇 시에 출발할 거예요?
lines.en: I've always wanted to go — perfect timing! What time are we leaving?

lines.speaker: 친구 A
lines.text: 오전 9시 어때요? 점심은 양평 해장국 먹을 데 찾아뒀어요.
lines.en: How about 9 AM? I already found a hangover soup place in Yangpyeong for lunch.

lines.speaker: 친구 B
lines.text: 완전 좋아요~ 같이 갈 사람 또 있어요?
lines.en: Sounds perfect! Is anyone else coming along?

lines.speaker: 친구 A
lines.text: 아직 없는데 더 불러도 될 것 같아요!
lines.en: Not yet — feel free to invite more people!

adult_dialogue.note_ko: 친구 사이지만 존댓말 — 처음 사귄 성인들이 자연스럽게 존댓말을 쓰는 상황이에요.
adult_dialogue.note_en: Friends, but using polite speech — the normal default between adults until they explicitly agree to use casual speech.

banmal_jondaemal.tip: 💡 어른 → 아이: 반말 / 아이 → 어른: 존댓말 — 이번 에피소드 전체에 흐르는 패턴!

pronunciation.title: 꽂은 → [꼬즌] · 연음화
pronunciation.rule: 받침 ㅈ + 모음 → ㅈ이 다음 음절로 이동해요.
pronunciation.rule_en: When a ㅈ final consonant is followed by a vowel, it moves to the next syllable.

pronunciation.title: 살아 있어요 → [사라이써요] · 연음 체인
pronunciation.rule_en: Consecutive final consonants all link forward to the next vowel.

pronunciation.title: 늙었잖아요 → [늘겄자나요] · 겹받침 연음
pronunciation.rule: 겹받침 ㄺ은 모음 앞에서 뒤 자음(ㄱ)이 다음 음절로 연음돼요.
pronunciation.rule_en: The double final consonant ㄺ: before a vowel, the second consonant (ㄱ) moves to the next syllable.

usage.section: 🏯 한국 사찰 방문 팁 · Visiting a Korean Buddhist Temple

items.ko: 입장 전에 일주문(一柱門) 을 지나요. 속세와 성역의 경계를 나타내는 문이에요.
items.en: You pass through the 일주문 (single-pillar gate) — it marks the boundary between the ordinary world and the sacred space.

items.ko: 법당(불상이 있는 건물) 앞에서는 합장하고 허리를 숙여 인사해요.
items.en: In front of the main hall (where the Buddha is), press your palms together and bow.

items.ko: 조용히 걷고, 큰 소리는 삼가야 해요. 명상하는 스님들이 계실 수 있어요.
items.en: Walk quietly and avoid loud noise — monks may be meditating nearby.

items.ko: 용문사는 경기도 양평에 있어요. 서울에서 기차로 약 1시간 거리예요.
items.en: Yongmunsa is in Yangpyeong, Gyeonggi-do — about 1 hour from Seoul by train.
I'm hungry — what should we eat? (background → suggestion)
I'm studying right now — I'll call you later. (background → explanation)
This is delicious — give it a try! (positive background → recommendation)
The weather is nice — shall we go for a walk? (background → invitation)

usage.section: 🌳 천연기념물 이야기 · Stories of Natural Monuments

items.ko: 한국에는 오래된 나무, 동굴, 동물 등을 보호하는 '천연기념물' 제도가 있어요.
items.en: Korea has a 'Natural Monument' designation system that protects old trees, caves, animals, and more.

items.ko: 용문사 은행나무는 제30호. 높이 42m, 둘레 14m — 한국에서 가장 큰 은행나무예요.
items.en: Yongmunsa's ginkgo tree is Natural Monument No. 30. At 42m tall and 14m around — the largest ginkgo in Korea.

items.ko: 한국에는 비슷한 전설을 가진 오래된 나무들이 많아요. 나무는 마을의 수호신으로 여겨지기도 했어요.
items.en: Korea has many ancient trees with similar legends — trees were often considered guardian spirits of a village.

quiz.question_en: How old is the ginkgo tree?
What does '갈' do in '같이 갈 사람?'
quiz.explanation: 갈 사람 = a person who will go. -(으)ㄹ+N은 미래·예정 동작으로 뒤의 명사를 수식해요.

quiz.audio_text: 은행나무한테 부탁할 거야. 우리 할머니 할아버지를 지켜줘.
quiz.question_en: Who did Liam make a request to?

quiz.explanation: 둘 다 '누구에게'를 의미하지만, -한테는 일상 대화에서, -에게는 공식적인 글이나 말에서 써요.

real_life.title: 🌳 천 년의 나무 — 용문사 은행나무
real_life.passage: 경기도 양평에 있는 용문사에는 세계적으로도 드문 1,100년 이상 된 은행나무가 있어요. 높이 42미터, 둘레 14미터로, 나라에서 지정한 천연기념물 제30호예요. 신라의 마지막 왕자 마의 태자가 나라를 잃은 슬픔으로 금강산으로 가던 중 이 자리에 지팡이를 꽂았다는 이야기가 전해져요. 6.25 전쟁 때도 불을 피했다는 신비로운 나무예요.

questions.question_en: What number Natural Monument is this ginkgo tree?
Where was Prince Maui heading when he planted the stick?

messages.speaker: 친구
messages.text: 용문사에 가는데 같이 갈래요? 은행나무 보고 싶어서요.

messages.speaker: 친구
messages.text: 오전 9시 어때요? 점심은 양평 해장국 먹을 곳 찾아뒀어요.

messages.speaker: 나
messages.text: 완전 좋아요~ 같이 갈 사람 또 있어요?

messages.speaker: 친구
messages.text: 아직 없는데 더 불러도 될 것 같아요!

self_check.en: I can make -(으)ㄹ+N phrases like 'person who will go' and 'something to eat.'

self_check.ko: -는데로 배경을 설명하고 부탁하거나 질문할 수 있어요.
self_check.en: I can use -는데 to give background and then make a request or ask a question.

self_check.ko: -한테/-에게를 써서 누구에게 말하는지 표현할 수 있어요.
self_check.en: I can use -한테/-에게 to show who I'm talking to or giving something to.

self_check.ko: 늙다 와 오래되다 의 차이를 알아요 (사람 vs 사물).
self_check.en: I know the difference between 늙다 (for people) and 오래되다 (for things).

self_check.ko: 용문사 은행나무 이야기를 한국어로 설명할 수 있어요.
self_check.en: I can tell the story of the Yongmunsa ginkgo tree in Korean.
